commit.message:
Step 4: Work cycle orchestration workflows (#6)

* Add work cycle orchestration workflows (Step 4)

Implement GitHub Actions workflows for all five work cycles, wired for
Phase 1 agents. Each workflow triggers on the appropriate event and
invokes the right agents in order via a shared reusable template.

New files:
- reusable/agent-invoke.yml: shared template handling persona loading,
  stage label management, tracking comments, and Claude Code invocation
- loop-planning.yml: triggered on ticket merge, runs Tech Lead -> PM review
- loop-verification.yml: triggered on milestone activation, runs
  Tech Lead -> Developer -> Acceptance Tester -> Tech Lead -> PM review
- loop-implementation.yml: triggered on verification/acceptance merge,
  runs Developer (2 stages) -> Tech Lead review -> AT review -> PM review
- loop-accept.yml: triggered on implementation merge, runs
  Developer -> Acceptance Tester -> Tech Lead -> PM review
- loop-improve.yml: triggered on milestone completion, runs
  Scrum Master -> PM review -> Tech Lead review
- loop-on-review.yml: handles PR review events for the rejection flow,
  advancing or reverting stages based on approve/request-changes

All workflows share a concurrency group (agent-execution) ensuring
serialized execution. Stage sequences match the definitions in
docs/agents/work-cycles/*.md.

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

* Rename loop-* workflows to work-cycle-*

Rename all workflow files from 'loop-' prefix to 'work-cycle-' prefix
to match the terminology used throughout the project. Update references
in OWNERS.yaml, PLAN.md, and the scrum-master persona definition.

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

* Extract work cycle logic into testable Python scripts

Add scripts/work-cycle/ with 7 standalone Python modules:
- detect_ticket.py: find new/changed tickets from git diff
- detect_milestone.py: find activated (in-progress) milestones
- detect_next_pr.py: find the next planned PR to implement
- detect_impl_pr.py: find plan context for a merged implementation PR
- detect_completed_milestone.py: find newly completed milestones
- render_tracking_comment.py: render PR progress tracking table
- render_prompt.py: substitute {{variable}} placeholders in templates

Each script has a corresponding test file with comprehensive coverage
(60 tests total). All scripts are importable as libraries and also
work as CLI tools for GitHub Actions shell steps.

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

* Extract prompt templates into standalone markdown files

Add docs/agents/prompts/ with 21 template files organized by work cycle:
- _shared/contribute.md: wrapper for all contribute stage prompts
- _shared/review.md: wrapper for all review stage prompts
- planning/: 2 task-specific templates
- verification/: 5 task-specific templates
- implementation/: 5 task-specific templates
- accept/: 4 task-specific templates
- improve/: 3 task-specific templates

Templates use {{variable}} syntax for substitution via
scripts/work-cycle/render_prompt.py. This makes prompts easy to
review, edit, and version independently of workflow YAML.

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

* Rewrite workflows to use extracted scripts and prompt templates

Update all work cycle workflows and the reusable agent-invoke template
to call Python scripts for detection logic and load prompt templates
from docs/agents/prompts/ via render_prompt.py.

Changes to agent-invoke.yml:
- Replace inline prompt input with prompt_template + prompt_vars
- Use render_tracking_comment.py for PR progress table rendering
- Use render_prompt.py for both task-specific and wrapper prompts
- Add Python setup step for script dependencies

Changes to loop workflows:
- Replace inline Python/bash detection logic with script calls
- Replace inline prompt strings with template path + variables
- All prompt content now lives in docs/agents/prompts/

Changes to OWNERS.yaml:
- Add scripts/work-cycle/** to scrum-master ownership

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

* Extract remaining inline logic into external scripts

Move all remaining testable logic out of workflow YAML:

Python:
- classify_pr.py: classify merged PRs by branch name and labels
  (replaces inline bash grep patterns in implementation + accept)
  12 tests covering branch patterns, label matching, edge cases

JavaScript (for actions/github-script):
- github/manage-stage-labels.js: add/remove stage/* labels on PRs
- github/upsert-tracking-comment.js: create or update progress comment
- github/request-human-review.js: post human review request comment
- github/create-work-cycle-pr.js: create a PR with work-cycle label
- github/handle-review-event.js: process review approval/rejection

Workflow YAML now contains only GHA orchestration glue:
- Dependency install one-liners (pip install)
- Git branch creation (checkout + push)
- Thin wrappers piping script output to GITHUB_OUTPUT
- Static env vars passed to external JS scripts

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

* Add declarative stage definition YAML files

Create docs/agents/work-cycles/stages/ with one YAML file per work cycle:
- planning.yaml: 2 stages (Tech Lead contribute, PM review)
- verification.yaml: 5 stages (TL, Dev, AT, TL, PM)
- implementation.yaml: 5 stages (Dev x2, TL review, AT review, PM review)
- accept.yaml: 4 stages (Dev, AT, TL, PM review)
- improve.yaml: 3 stages (SM, PM review, TL review)

Each file declaratively defines:
- Trigger configuration (push/pull_request, branches, paths)
- Detection logic (script, outputs, run conditions)
- Branch naming (prefix, suffix, fallbacks)
- PR creation (after which stage, title, body, label)
- Stage sequence (persona, type, label, prompt template, prompt vars)

These files are the single source of truth for work cycle stage
sequences. The workflow YAML files are generated from them.

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

* Add workflow generator script with tests

Create generate_workflows.py that reads stage definitions from
docs/agents/work-cycles/stages/*.yaml and generates the corresponding
GitHub Actions workflow files.

The generator handles:
- Trigger configuration (push with path filters, pull_request with types)
- Detection jobs with optional Python setup and fetch depth
- Branch creation jobs with prefix/suffix/fallback patterns
- Sequential stage jobs with proper needs chains
- PR creation jobs inserted at the right point in the chain
- all_stages_json computation from stage definitions
- Generated header with source file reference

28 tests covering:
- slugify, trigger building, detect job construction
- Branch job variants (prefix, from_output, fallback)
- Stage job construction (contribute, review, needs dedup)
- Full workflow generation (minimal, with branch+PR)
- Integration tests against real stage files

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

* Replace handwritten workflows with generated output

Regenerate all 5 work-cycle workflow files from stage definitions.
The generated files are functionally equivalent to the handwritten
versions but produced by generate_workflows.py.

Each file now has an AUTO-GENERATED header referencing its source
stage definition file and a reminder to use 'make generate-workflows'.

Add Makefile targets:
- generate-workflows: regenerate from stage definitions
- check-workflows: verify generated files are up to date (for CI)
- test-work-cycle: run the work cycle script test suite

Co-authored-by: Yuri Shewchuk <ycshewchuk@gmail.com>

commit.message:
feat(rust-sdk): Add on_tick streaming callback with FunctionLog support (#3333)

## Summary

- Adds `on_tick` callback support to the Rust SDK streaming API,
matching Go/Python behavior
- The callback receives `&FunctionLog` on each SSE chunk, giving access
to thinking tokens, SSE responses, usage, and timing data
- Calling `.with_on_tick(|log| { ... })` automatically creates an
internal collector (just like Go's `WithOnTick`) — no manual collector
setup needed

## Design

The callback and its auto-created collector are bundled into `OnTickData
{ callback, collector }`, which flows through the system as a single
unit:

1. **Generated code** (`with_on_tick` in `FunctionOptions`) creates a
collector, adds it to the collectors list, and wraps both into
`OnTickData`
2. **SDK** (`FunctionArgs`) carries the `OnTickData` to the runtime
3. **FFI callbacks** (`callbacks.rs`) on each SSE chunk, queries the
collector for the latest `FunctionLog` and passes it to the user's
callback

The collector is never exposed to the user.

## Usage

```rust
let mut stream = B.MyFunction
    .with_on_tick(|log: &baml::FunctionLog| {
        for call in log.calls() {
            if let Some(stream_call) = call.as_stream() {
                if let Some(chunks) = stream_call.sse_chunks() {
                    for chunk in &chunks {
                        println!("thinking token: {}", chunk.text());
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    })
    .stream(args)
    .expect("Failed to start stream");
```

## Files Changed

**Rust SDK (`languages/rust/baml/src/`)**
- `ffi/callbacks.rs` — New `OnTickData` struct bundling callback +
collector; simplified `create_callback_with_on_tick` to take single
`Option<OnTickData>`
- `args.rs` — `FunctionArgs.on_tick` is now `Option<OnTickData>`;
removed separate `on_tick_collector` field
- `runtime.rs` — Passes single `on_tick` to callback registration
- `lib.rs` — Exports `OnTickData`

**Code Generator (`engine/generators/languages/rust/src/_templates/`)**
- `runtime.rs.j2` — `FunctionOptions.with_on_tick()` auto-creates
collector and bundles into `OnTickData`; removed `on_tick_collector`
field
- `sync_client.rs.j2` / `async_client.rs.j2` — Convenience macro
delegates to `FunctionOptions.with_on_tick()`

**Tests (`engine/generators/data/sample/rust/main.rs`)**
- 4 on_tick tests (sync/async, with/without explicit collector) — all
print thinking tokens from `FunctionLog`

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test --lib` passes for `baml` crate (SDK unit tests)
- [x] `RUN_GENERATOR_TESTS=1 cargo test -p generators-rust --lib --
sample_evaluate` passes (generated code compiles and on_tick tests work)
- [x] Tests demonstrate printing thinking tokens from SSE chunks via
`FunctionLog`

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Streaming "on-tick" callbacks: user callbacks are invoked for each SSE
streaming chunk.
* Builder methods to configure per-call tick callbacks on both sync and
async client APIs.
* Automatic collector injection when tick callbacks are used, enabling
per-chunk logs.

* **Tests**
* Added sync and async streaming tests validating tick callbacks,
partial stream handling, final responses, and collector integration.

commit.message:
fix(ppu): sprite OAM index priority on overlaps (SRE-6) (#13)

* fix(ppu): correct sprite OAM index priority on overlaps

Hardware composites sprites in OAM order; lower index wins when pixels
overlap. decode_obj overwrites per-pixel data, so return scanline sprites
with higher OAM index first (after still selecting the first 32 in index
order). Add a unit test for the iteration order.

Co-authored-by: Dennis Kempin <denniskempin@google.com>

* test(ppu): refresh golden images for sprite OAM priority fix

Framebuffer comparisons for scenes with overlapping sprites now match
correct compositing (lower OAM index in front).

Co-authored-by: Dennis Kempin <denniskempin@google.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat(rest): Add `content` property to RestEndpoint for typed response parsing (#3868)

* feat(rest): add content property for response parsing and binary auto-detection

Add a 'content' string property to RestEndpoint that controls response
parsing ('json' | 'blob' | 'text' | 'arrayBuffer' | 'stream'), infers
return types via ContentTypeMap, and constrains schema compatibility via
ContentSchemaGuard -- all without explicit generic parameters.

When content is not set, auto-detect binary Content-Types using a compact
regex and return response.blob() instead of corrupting data via .text().

Runtime schema check throws when binary content is used with a
normalizable schema, matching the existing pattern in parseResponse.

Co-authored-by: natmaster <natmaster@gmail.com>

image/png, application/octet-stream, application/pdf, text/plain,
application/json, application/xml, and missing Content-Type.

Type tests: return type inference (Blob, string, ArrayBuffer,
ReadableStream), schema constraint errors, extend type propagation,
process priority over content, subclass pattern.

Add content section to RestEndpoint.md with table of values, return

feat: add public API surface + export bundle (#1027)

* fix(llm): resolve conflicts - use model variable for token params in shadow-eval; fix requiresMaxCompletionTokens

* fix(shadow-eval): resolve conflict - keep single chatCompletionTokenParams call

* feat(public-api): resolve merge conflicts - keep new public API surface

* Fix: Map link_type to linkType in getAllEdges for PublicExportBundle API contract

* Fix: Update dashboard-graph.ts to use linkType instead of link_type

The getAllEdges function in clusters.ts returns linkType (camelCase),
but dashboard-graph.ts was still accessing link_type (snake_case).
This caused link_type to be undefined in the graph payload, breaking
the D3 visualization edge coloring. Updated the MemoryGraphEdge
interface and all references to use linkType consistently.

* fix(tool-installers): resolve conflict - keep closing brace

* fix: optimize timeline and export endpoints to avoid loading all facts into memory

Replace getAll() with list() in timeline and export endpoints to apply LIMIT at SQL level instead of loading all facts into memory and slicing. This prevents full table scans and reduces memory pressure as the facts database grows.

* fix: address Council review comments on public API surface

- Fix P1: register routes with SDK route object API {path, auth, handler}
  instead of legacy 3-arg (path, handler, opts) signature
- Fix P1: wrap decodeURIComponent in try/catch to return 400 on
  malformed percent-encoding instead of bubbling 500
- Fix P2: normalize getAllEdges snake_case links to camelCase
  linkType in public export bundle
- Fix P2: use factsDb.list(limit, {includeSuperseded:false}) in
  /timeline to avoid full-scan + in-memory sort
- Fix medium: use factsDb.list(factsLimit) in buildPublicExportBundle
  instead of getAll() + in-memory slice
- Fix low: remove unused 'vi' import from provider-router.test.ts
- Fix low: remove silent error swallow in safeLoadNarratives

* Fix makeRoute to call registerHttpRoute with correct signature

The makeRoute helper was incorrectly casting registerHttpRoute to accept
a single object argument, causing all public API routes to fail registration.
Fixed to use the correct three-argument signature (path, handler, opts)
matching the SDK API and dashboard-routes.ts pattern.

* Fix: Remove unsupported includeSuperseded filter from timeline endpoint

The list() method does not accept includeSuperseded as a filter option.
Superseded facts are already excluded by default in the listFacts implementation,
so removing this parameter maintains the correct behavior while fixing the
misleading API usage.

* fix: extract duplicated HTTP route types to shared module

Consolidates HttpRouteOptions and HttpRequestHandler types from
public-api-routes.ts and dashboard-routes.ts into a new shared
http-route-types.ts module to eliminate code duplication and
ensure consistent maintenance of the registerHttpRoute contract.

* test: add optional:publicApi to installer ordering test

* style: apply biome formatting to public-api-routes.ts and tool-installers.ts

The Biome formatter (v1.9.4) was flagging multi-line makeRoute() calls
for not condensing to single-line when they fit. This resolves the
Format Check (Biome) CI failure on PR #1040.

* Fix missing adapter functions for public API tool installer

Add selectPublicApiRoutesContext and installPublicApiRoutes wrapper functions that were referenced but not defined, preventing ReferenceError at module load time.

fix(rest): Bundle path-to-regexp into ESM output for StackBlitz compat (#3866)

* fix(rest): Bundle path-to-regexp into ESM output for StackBlitz compat

StackBlitz WebContainers fundamentally cannot resolve CJS named exports
from path-to-regexp, regardless of import style (named, namespace, etc).

Add a rollup ESM build (dist/esm/index.js) that bundles path-to-regexp
directly, eliminating the CJS boundary. Point the "module" and "browser"
export conditions to this bundled ESM output. CJS and React Native paths
are unchanged.

Reverts the namespace import workaround since it's no longer needed.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(rest): Handle any number of chained CJS void-initializations in pathToRegexpESM

The regex that strips CJS `exports.X = void 0` lines only matched exactly
two chained assignments. TypeScript CJS output can chain all exports on one
line (e.g. 7+ assignments). The old regex would leave the line intact,
causing a ReferenceError in ESM where `exports` is undefined.

Use `/^(?:exports\.\w+\s*=\s*)+void 0;\s*\n/gm` which matches one or more
chained `exports.X =` followed by `void 0`.

Co-authored-by: Nathaniel Tucker <me@ntucker.me>

* fix(rest): dev script passes -w to babel instead of rollup

The build:lib script chains babel && rollup. When dev ran 'run build:lib -w',
the -w flag was appended to the last command (rollup), so babel never watched
for changes. Fix by inlining the babel command directly in the dev script.

Co-authored-by: Nathaniel Tucker <me@ntucker.me>
